game_name: Princess Tomato in the Salad Kingdom

danger_signals.meaning: Primary threat to kingdom stability; coup instigator

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy characters aligned with Minister Pumpkin
danger_signals.meaning: Organized hostile force supporting the coup
Physical barrier and danger zone where princess is held captive
Combat system; only way to lose the game; represents direct conflict

safety_signals.meaning: Player agency; hero role; represents order and rescue mission
Organized faction opposing the coup; allied support structure
Supportive presence; narrative companionship during quest
Legitimate authority figure; moral justification for restoring order

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: General kingdom territory; Orange Park; implied peaceful zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pumpkin as faction leader; 'Farmies' as enemy class
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hostile food/vegetable characters distinct from friendly NPCs

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Operating throughout kingdom territory; decentralized presence implied

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: King Broccoli's reign; peaceful Salad Kingdom depicted through narrative
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kingdom inhabitants lived as anthropomorphic foods in peaceful coexistence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Princess Tomato kidnapped and imprisoned in Zucchini Mountains; Minister Pumpkin in control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Resistance movement organized; King Broccoli dead from grief; urgent rescue mission framing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Princess Tomato rescued; legitimate order restored; Sir Cucumber rewarded with marriage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kingdom stability implied; succession through marriage alliance

environmental_storytelling.example: Anthropomorphic fruits and vegetables as inhabitants communicate the whimsical, food-themed setting; establishes tone and visual identity of Salad Kingdom
Zucchini Mountains, Orange Park, and Salad Kingdom signs communicate geography and danger zones; regional identity through vegetable-themed nomenclature
Japanese version included 'Salad Kingdom' (サラダの国) sign at top border; removal in US localization reflects different narrative clarity needs
Prince Lettuce leadership of resistance, Percy as sidekick, other allies met during adventure communicate political factional structure and support network
3D maze and navigational challenges in adventure screens represent physical danger and environmental hostility of coup-controlled zones

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Orchestrated coup; kidnapped princess; holds fortress position; engages in Finger Wars combat as final boss
Travels through dangerous terrain; follows fixed command menu interface; engages in Finger Wars combat; seeks to restore legitimate rule
Died of grief over kidnapping; delegated rescue mission to Cucumber before death; provides moral authority for resistance
Held captive in Zucchini Mountains; motivation object for entire quest; promised as reward for successful rescue
Leads organized resistance force against coup; provides faction structure; supports protagonist's mission
Serve Minister Pumpkin; represent organized opposition force; encountered as combat obstacles in adventure screens

notes: Princess Tomato uses whimsical food-based character design as primary world-state communication method. The game's text-adventure interface (with button-based commands) limits visual animation, so danger/safety/faction identity relies heavily on character design, NPC dialog, and environmental naming. The political situation (coup + rescue) is communicated through plot exposition rather than dynamic visual manifestation. The only genuine failure/loss state is losing the Finger Wars (rock-paper-scissors) final combat against Minister Pumpkin, making this the singular danger-peak. World-state progression is linear: peaceful kingdom → coup occurs → rescue mission underway → victory restores order. Regional differences (sign removal, text changes) suggest localization adapted environmental storytelling for North American audiences. Game mechanics center on static screens and fixed command menus, limiting dynamic behavioral tells to specific encounters.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Princess Tomato article)
